Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Marcus Webb departure-related threads using search_emails (query: "Marcus Webb" or "Marcus" or "resignation" or "departure" or similar) to find Marcus's resignation email to Grace, Raj's findings about late-night email forwards from Marcus's Keystone account to a personal Gmail, Denise's non-compete consultation with employment counsel Laura Bennett, and borrower transfer request emails from Abigail Clark, Stephen Hamilton, Keisha Perry, and Joshua Cruz.
OE 2: Search Slack messages for Marcus departure discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Marcus" or "Webb" or "forwarding" or "pipeline" or similar) to find Raj's report about four manual email forwards from Marcus's mailbox the night before his resignation, pipeline handoff discussions in #pipeline-ops, Marcus's notice announcement in #pipeline-ops ("Just sent Grace my notice"), and internal discussions about the data exfiltration concern in #it-security.
OE 3: Search the Mortgage LOS for Marcus Webb's former loan pipeline. Since mortgage_los_search_loans searches by borrower name, loan number, property address, or lender (not by loan officer name), the agent would need to discover the relevant loan numbers from other sources first (email threads, CRM engagements, Slack discussions, or Stripe transfer descriptions) and then use mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00008" or similar) to pull individual loan details. Alternatively, mortgage_los_get_pipeline with no filter or mortgage_los_list_staff could help surface the data. The agent should discover loans including LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00009, LN-2026-00211, LN-2026-00525, LN-2026-00527, and LN-2026-00608 and determine which have been reassigned and which may still be in transition.
OE 4: Get individual loan details for Marcus's loans using mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00008" or similar) or mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id to check current status, assigned loan officer, conditions, and any compliance flags on each file. The agent should discover the conflict of interest across three loans (LN-2026-00629, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00227) where Marcus's wife Danielle Webb served as the buyer's agent. Denise flagged this in email and CRM, noting particular concern about LN-2026-00211 due to an appraisal shortfall where Marcus's guidance could be viewed as influenced by household financial interest.
OE 5: Search CRM for Marcus Webb's deals and borrower engagement history using crm_search_deals (the agent may search by loan number in the dealname field, e.g., dealname: "LN-2026-00008" or similar, since "Marcus" does not appear in the dealname field; it only appears in deal descriptions as "LO: Marcus Webb") and crm_list_engagements to find deal statuses, whether any deals stalled during the handoff, engagement notes about the transition, and any logged complaints or escalations from borrowers who requested transfers.
OE 6: Search QuickBooks for any compensation or payable records related to Marcus Webb using quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria-based search). The agent is unlikely to find Marcus-specific commission records in QuickBooks, as there are no Marcus-related vendor records, invoices, or bills in the system. The primary commission evidence is in Stripe (see OE 7), where five active transfers to a departed-LO connected account (acct_scenario_644_departed / "Marcus Webb Consulting") indicate commissions continued flowing after his departure, which is a significant offboarding gap the agent should surface.
OE 7: Search Stripe for commission-related transfers and payment records using stripe_list_transfers or stripe_search_charges to verify there are no ongoing financial transfers tied to Marcus or his loans. The agent may discover that commission transfers to Marcus's connected account (acct_scenario_644_departed / "Marcus Webb Consulting") continued after his departure, including transfers tied to LN-2026-00525, LN-2026-00527, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00608.
OE 8: Search emails and CRM for borrower transfer request outcomes using search_emails (query: "transfer" or "Abigail Clark" or "Stephen Hamilton" or "Keisha Perry" or "Joshua Cruz" or similar) and crm_search_deals or crm_list_engagements to determine whether the four borrowers requesting file transfers were retained by Keystone or lost. At least one borrower (Joshua Cruz) emailed Jasmine Brooks directly rather than Grace, which the agent needs to discover.
OE 9: Search emails, CRM, and Slack for referral partner impact using search_emails (query: "referral" or referral partner names or similar), crm_search_companies, and conversations_search_messages to assess whether any realtor referral relationships were damaged by Marcus's departure or the borrower transfer situation.
OE 10: Search Slack and emails for Aiden Park's role resolution using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Aiden" or "Park" or similar) and search_emails to determine whether Aiden's reporting structure and daily responsibilities have been reassigned since Marcus was his direct supervisor and mentor. Aiden's role during the transition is uncertain. His job description is "Loan Officer Assistant" focused on pre-qualification support and lead intake, and Marcus was his primary supervisor. The agent should search Slack and email to determine whether his reporting line and responsibilities have been reassigned.
OE 11: Look up contact information for Denise Holloway and Grace Yamamoto using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Denise Holloway") and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Grace Yamamoto") to confirm their email addresses (denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com and grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com, or similar) for sending the status review emails.
OE 12: Send email to Denise Holloway using send_email (sender: r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com, recipients: [denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com or discovered email]) with the data handling and compliance section of the status review covering: data forwarding investigation status and findings, whether breach notification obligations have been met, the conflict of interest disclosure concern on Marcus's loans involving Danielle Webb as buyer's agent (if discovered), and non-compete enforcement status with outside counsel.
OE 13: Send email to Grace Yamamoto using send_email (sender: r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com, recipients: [grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com or discovered email]) with the operational section of the status review covering: pipeline reassignment status for each of Marcus's loans, any loans that stalled or have outstanding issues, borrower retention outcomes, referral partner impact assessment, and whether Aiden Park's role and reporting structure have been sorted.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

outcome
 ✓✗✓✗✓✓ 67%
✓Run #1The email to Denise explicitly states: '4 manual email forwards from Marcus's Keystone mailbox to a personal email address' and discusses data exfiltration, bulk downloads, and the unresolved breach notification determination.
✗Run #2The email to Denise does not reference Marcus forwarding data to a personal email address or unauthorized data exports by Marcus. The email mentions the borrower solicitation pattern and whether Marcus 'took borrower PII,' and references the Blue Peak/Raj Anand data export issue, but that is about Raj—not about Marcus forwarding data to a personal Gmail account, which was the specific concern Denise raised.
✓Run #3The Denise email explicitly references Blue Peak Security's findings about 'recurring borrower data exports observed weekly over a 6-week window' using Raj Anand's credentials from an out-of-state IP — directly addressing unauthorized data export by Marcus prior to resignation.
✗Run #4The email to Denise references borrower data concerns (Raj/data leaving the LOS) and post-departure solicitation by Marcus, but does not mention Marcus forwarding data to a personal email address or personal Gmail. The agent found no evidence of personal email forwarding in its search, and the email to Denise does not reference this specific type of data exfiltration.
✓Run #5The email to Denise explicitly states: 'Raj's initial sweep found 4 manual email forwards from Marcus's mailbox to a personal address the night before or around his departure, plus bulk opens/downloads across pipeline sheets and 2 active loan summaries.' This clearly references data forwarding to a personal email address.
✓Run #6The Denise email explicitly references '4 manual email forwards from Marcus's mailbox to a personal address' and bulk opens/downloads of pipeline sheets, directly addressing the data forwarding concern.
4 The email to [denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com](mailto:denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com) (visible in send_email parameters) references Danielle Webb as Marcus Webb's wife and the undisclosed conflict of interest involving her role as buyer's agent on Marcus's loan files.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to Denise includes a 'DANIELLE WEBB CONFLICT OF INTEREST' section explicitly identifying Danielle Webb as Marcus's wife, noting she was the buyer's agent on three active files with no disclosed relationship.
✓Run #2The email to Denise explicitly states: 'You identified Marcus had an undisclosed conflict on 3 active files where his wife Danielle Webb was the buyer's agent, with particular concern on LN-2026-00211 and the appraisal gap.' This clearly references Danielle Webb as Marcus's wife and the undisclosed conflict of interest.
✓Run #3The Denise email explicitly states 'Three files had Marcus originating loans where his wife Danielle Webb was the buyer's agent' and identifies the undisclosed spousal relationship as a conflict of interest across LN-2026-00629, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00227.
✓Run #4The email to Denise explicitly references 'Danielle Webb (Marcus's wife, per LinkedIn)' and states she was the buyer's agent on three active files, describing it as an 'UNDISCLOSED CONFLICT OF INTEREST.' This clearly addresses the spousal relationship and the undisclosed conflict.
✓Run #5The email to Denise explicitly names 'Danielle Webb — his wife — was the buyer's agent' on three active files with no disclosure on file, directly identifying the spousal relationship and undisclosed conflict of interest.
✓Run #6The Denise email contains an entire section titled 'UNDISCLOSED CONFLICT OF INTEREST — DANIELLE WEBB,' explicitly identifying Danielle Webb as Marcus's wife and the buyer's agent on 3 files without disclosure.
5 The email to [denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com](mailto:denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com) (visible in send_email parameters) specifically identifies LN-2026-00227 as a loan that closed without a conflict-of-interest disclosure on file.
outcome
 ✓✗✓✓✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1The email to Denise specifically references 'LN-2026-00227 (Tiffany Wright, USDA purchase) -- closed on 3/21' and asks whether there is RESPA exposure on a closed loan with an undisclosed affiliated business arrangement.
✗Run #2The email to Denise does not specifically identify LN-2026-00227 as the loan that closed without a conflict-of-interest disclosure. The email mentions LN-2026-00211 and '3 active files' but never references LN-2026-00227, the closed loan with the most critical RESPA exposure.
✓Run #3The Denise email specifically calls out LN-2026-00227 as having 'Closed (assigned to Amy Chen)' in the context of the undisclosed conflict, and asks whether retroactive disclosure or remediation is needed. The loan number appears explicitly.
✓Run #4The email to Denise explicitly mentions 'LN-2026-00227: Closed on 3/21. Title had requested commission split details before CD prep — unclear whether the spousal relationship was disclosed on the closing documents.' This identifies that LN-2026-00227 closed without a confirmed conflict-of-interest disclosure on file.
